text.title: Pricing Research Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Capture-rate priors by category, annual-discount math trap, currency localization, three-agent separation rule, and human-in-loop checkpoints.

antipattern.description: Agent suggests 10-15% capture rate regardless of product category or market context.
antipattern.reason: The 10-20% folk number appears frequently in startup content. LLMs apply it uniformly without category adjustment.

rule.statement: Override capture rate with category-specific priors: devtools 1-3%, productivity 5-10%, vertical SaaS 15-25%. Apply these as hard overrides when the agent suggests a generic range.
rule.rationale: Category-specific priors are derived from actual SaaS benchmarks (OpenView, ProfitWell). Generic 10-20% systematically overestimates devtools pricing potential and underestimates vertical SaaS.

antipattern.description: Agent computes annual price as "12 x monthly x 0.83" (17% off) instead of "10 x monthly" (2 months free).
antipattern.reason: Percentage-discount framing triggers percentage math. The "2 months free" framing should produce 10 x monthly but agents revert to percentage calculation.

rule.statement: Force the formula in the prompt: annual_price = monthly_price x 10. Do not describe it as a percentage discount.
rule.rationale: Explicit formula prevents the recalculation error. The math is identical to "2 months free" and "16.7% off" but the agent will compute it correctly only when given the multiplier form.

rule.statement: Use three separate agents for competitor scraping, value modeling, and tier design. Never let a single agent do all three — pricing logic compounds errors across stages.
rule.rationale: Each stage has a different fabrication risk. A single agent accumulates errors from competitor confabulation into value modeling, then into tier recommendations. Separation provides natural checkpoints.

rule.statement: Serve EUR/INR/BRL prices adjusted for PPP, not 1:1 USD conversion. Agents routinely miss regional pricing adjustments and produce USD-only recommendations.
rule.rationale: Flat USD pricing in markets with significantly lower purchasing power dramatically reduces conversion. PPP-adjusted bands are standard for SaaS products targeting global markets.

rule.statement: Actual price commits (creating Stripe price objects, publishing pricing page) are human decisions. Agent output is "recommended bands"; the button-press is not delegated.
rule.rationale: Pricing errors are hard to reverse — raising prices costs customer trust; refunding grandfathered customers costs margin. The final decision requires human judgment that accounts for current business context.

list.item: Reject any Van Westendorp synthesis computed from fewer than 30 real respondents.
Reject any competitor feature row without a source_quote from the actual pricing page.
Mark all "contact us" tiers as negotiated and exclude from median calculations.
